content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Error-Aware Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation in LLMs",
  "problem": "LLMs often generate syntactically correct but functionally incorrect code, partly due to their inability to recognize and repair common error patterns during generation. Existing prompting methods fail to proactively guide the model around known failure modes.",
  "motivation": "Code generation LLMs are increasingly used for real-world development, but their output still requires extensive human correction. Understanding and mitigating frequent code generation errors can substantially improve productivity and reliability, especially as LLMs are deployed in professional coding workflows.",
  "method": "We propose a dynamic error-aware prompting method that augments input prompts with a list of common code generation error patterns mined from prior LLM generations on similar tasks (e.g., off-by-one errors, missing imports, incorrect function signatures). The prompt instructs the model to specifically avoid these patterns and, when possible, output self-checks or inline comments to verify correctness. To automate this, we cluster prior generations by error type, constructing prompt augmentations conditionally based on task similarity. This creates a feedback loop where the prompt evolves in response to the model's own failure distribution.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate this method on HumanEval, MBPP, and APPS datasets, comparing against standard instruction and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include pass@k, functional correctness, and frequency of recurring known errors. Additionally, we will perform ablation studies measuring the impact of dynamic error lists and prompt personalization."
}
